header.title: Cost & CLI usage

welcome.body: AI Launcher Pro detects your installed CLIs, switches providers in a click, and launches with the right environment — straight from your friendly dashboard.

detect.body: The real list appears in the Launcher tab after you finish the tour.

provider.body: You configure tokens later in Admin → Providers. Tokens stay local only, zero telemetry.

launch.body: Press <1>{{cmd}}</1> <2>{{k}}</2> to open the palette anytime, or go back to the Launcher tab and pick a CLI.

tour.title: 9 tabs, one keyboard
tour.body: Press → to skip ahead, ← to go back, enter to move on.

settings.refreshIntervalHint: Default: 0 (manual). Set to 60+ for background auto-refresh.
settings.commandTimeoutHint: Default: 30. Increase for slow networks or large installs.

welcome.firstTimeBody: Click "Validate System" for the launcher to detect Node, Python, Git, Rust, installed CLIs, etc. Takes a few seconds and unlocks all features.
welcome.hideCheckbox: Don't show this screen again (can re-enable in Help)

installTab.sub: Manage prerequisites and AI CLIs. Silent install with live log.

updatesTab.noComparisonTitle: No public API to compare version

helpTab.sub: Everything you need to know to use AI Launcher Pro.
helpTab.whatIsBody: Centralized hub to manage, install, update and launch AI CLIs (Claude, Codex, Gemini, Qwen, Kilo, OpenCode, Crush, Factory Droid) and dev tools (VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, AntGravity). All in one place, no flashing terminals, with live progress.
helpTab.installBody: Tab 📦 Install → "CLIs not installed" section → click "Install". Installation is silent in the background, with a live log shown below. Missing prerequisites (Node, Git, Python, etc.) have their own install button.
helpTab.factoryDroidBody: Factory Droid: uses the official script, native on Windows. When you click Install, the launcher runs the script via PowerShell in the background.
helpTab.launchStep1: Tab ⚡ Launcher → click the desired CLI card
helpTab.launchStep2: Choose the directory (Ctrl+L focuses the field)
helpTab.launchStep4: Click START (or Ctrl+K). Opens in Windows Terminal if available, else PowerShell, else cmd.
helpTab.launchMulti: To launch multiple, check boxes in the "Multiple CLIs" section and click the second button.
helpTab.updatesBody: Tab 🔔 Updates → "Check all" button. Queries in parallel:
helpTab.updatesListCli: CLIs: compares local version vs npm registry
helpTab.updatesListPrereqs: Prerequisites: local version + latest of npm packages (pnpm, yarn, tauri)
helpTab.updatesNote: Note: IDE updates (VS Code / Cursor / Windsurf) were removed — update inside the IDE itself.
helpTab.updatesBulk: When there are multiple CLI updates, use "Update all" to apply them in batch.
helpTab.securityBody1: If Windows Defender or SmartScreen flags the app: it's a typical false positive for new binaries. The app is signed with a self-signed cert (DevManiacs). To remove the alert on this machine, the cert has already been installed in the Trusted Root. On other machines, the user needs to import the .cer manually, OR the author needs to buy a real cert (Sectigo, DigiCert, SSL.com or Azure Trusted Signing).
helpTab.securityBody2: Every install/update now runs via tokio::process directly, without an intermediate cmd /C — this eliminates the "hidden process → invisible cmd → downloads from the internet" heuristic that triggered Defender.
helpTab.troubleshootItem1Title: A CLI appears "detected" but without version
helpTab.troubleshootItem1Body: The binary exists in PATH but the --version command returned unparseable output. Usually benign — the CLI works. Try running the command manually in the terminal to confirm.
helpTab.troubleshootItem2Body: See the live log on the Install tab (panel below). Persistent log in %APPDATA%/ai-launcher/install.log. Common causes: lack of permission for npm global, corporate proxy, Defender blocking npm.
helpTab.troubleshootItem3Body: The typed path doesn't exist on disk or was deleted. Use the Pick folder button to select a valid folder.
helpTab.welcomeBody: The screen appears whenever you open the app, unless you check "Don't show this screen again". You can re-enable it at any time:
helpTab.traySection: The app keeps an icon next to the clock. Left click opens/focuses the window; right click shows a menu with quick CLI launches and access to the tabs.
helpTab.trayMinimize: Minimize to tray when closing (instead of quitting)
helpTab.trayShortcutHelp: Format: modifiers separated by +. Ex: CommandOrControl+Shift+L, Alt+F1. Conflicts with other apps (Raycast, AutoHotkey) may block registration — choose another combination if that happens.
helpTab.onboardingBody: Review the welcome tour with shortcuts and guided install.
helpTab.resetBody: Removes: saved directory, history, theme, welcome preference, install log. The app reloads.
helpTab.supportBody: Developed by Helbert Moura. Brand DevManiac's.

whatIs.a: A desktop launcher that manages AI coding CLIs (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor…) from a single keyboard-first surface. Local-only, no telemetry.

whichCLIs.a: Eight built-in: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Qwen, iFlow, Copilot CLI, plus Kilo / Crush / Droid / OpenCode. Custom CLIs can be added from Admin → Add Custom CLI.

switchProvider.a: Open Admin → Providers, click 'Activate' on any profile. The active provider's base URL and API key are injected into the next CLI launch.

addProvider.q: Can I add a provider not in the list?
addProvider.a: Yes. Admin → Providers → '+ New profile'. Set `kind: custom` with the Anthropic-compatible base URL and paste your API key.

adminUnlock.q: How do I enable admin mode?
adminUnlock.a: Press ⌘⇧A (Ctrl+Shift+A on Windows/Linux). Or launch with ?admin=1 URL param. Or build from source with VITE_ADMIN_MODE=1.

tokensSafe.q: Where are my API tokens stored?
tokensSafe.a: In your browser's localStorage, local to this machine. Never transmitted to any third party except the provider you configured.

exportConfig.a: Admin → Backup → Export JSON. Secrets are redacted in the exported file; you re-enter them after import.

changeLanguage.a: Click the globe icon in the header, or press ⌘⇧L to cycle EN ↔ pt-BR.

firstProvider.q: Which provider should I start with?
firstProvider.a: If you have an Anthropic subscription, use Anthropic (default). For Chinese-friendly pricing, try Z.AI, Moonshot, or Qwen. For aggregator access, OpenRouter.

platforms.a: Windows 10/11 today. macOS and Linux builds are planned — follow the GitHub releases page.

fields.name: Display name
fields.iconHint: Use an emoji, 1-2 chars, or leave blank for default

validation.keyInvalid: Key must be lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes only

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.description: The app caught a rendering error. A log has been saved locally.

presets.saveHint: Save current combination as a preset
presets.namePlaceholder: Preset name (e.g. Refactor mode, Review daily, etc.)
presets.empty: No presets yet. Configure CLI + directory + arguments + provider, then click

providerSelector.title: PROVIDER (Claude)
providerSelector.overrideHint: Quick model override for this launch
providerSelector.capWarnText: Even if requesting "opus 1M" to Claude Code, the {{name}} backend truncates the context. Plan long conversations with this in mind.
